questions.question: Define a structure named 'Student' with members: name (character array of 30), rollNo (integer), and marks (float). Also show how to declare a variable of this structure.
Explain the difference between structures and arrays with examples.
keyPoints: Arrays store same type elements, structures store different types
Array uses index access, structure uses member operator
Example showing array of ints vs struct with int, float, char
Structure provides logical grouping for heterogeneous data

questions.question: What is structure padding? Explain why it occurs and how it affects the sizeof a structure.
keyPoints: Definition: extra bytes added between members
Reason: memory alignment for CPU efficiency

questions.question: What is a nested structure? Give an example showing how to declare and access members of a nested structure.
keyPoints: Example: Date structure inside Employee structure

questions.question: Explain the purpose of typedef with structures. How does it simplify code? Provide an example.
keyPoints: typedef creates an alias for structure type
Example showing with and without typedef

questions.question: Write a C program to declare a structure to store complex numbers (real and imaginary parts) and demonstrate initializing and displaying values.
keyPoints: Define struct Complex with float real, imag
Display using printf with member access

questions.question: Explain the difference between 'struct' keyword usage with and without typedef. Provide examples of declaring variables in both cases.
keyPoints: Without typedef: must use 'struct Tag var;'

questions.question: Define a structure named 'Book' with members: title (character array of 50), author (character array of 30), price (float), and year (integer). Write a program to: (a) Declare a variable of this structure, (b) Initialize it with values, (c) Display the values using the dot operator, (d) Calculate and display the total size using sizeof.

Explain the concept of structure padding with a detailed example. Given a structure with members: char a; int b; char c; on a system with 4-byte int alignment, calculate the actual size and explain the padding bytes. Also suggest how to minimize padding.
keyPoints: Show memory layout with byte numbers

questions.question: Create a structure to represent a point in 2D coordinate system with x and y coordinates. Then create another structure representing a circle that uses this point structure as its center and has a radius member. Write a program to declare a circle variable, initialize it, and calculate its area.
keyPoints: Define Point structure with float x, y
Define Circle structure with Point center, float radius
Access nested members using chained operator
